title: vicia sativa var. cordata
intro: Vicia sativa var. cordata, also known as narrow-leaved vetch or narrow-leaved pea, is a perennial legume plant that belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Vicia sativa var. cordata include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function. The plant can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being.
